title: Flaky test_provider_error_handling + unguarded awareness first-load can crash a turn
what_failed: test_429_gives_friendly_message failed in the 13:57 verify run but passes standalone and on re-run — flaky. The fixture builds a real PiAgent and only mocks router.chat; the first awareness_snapshot access does a SYNCHRONOUS live fetch (weather/news/stocks/geo-IP) with no try/except. A transient network failure escapes the property, is caught by the generic _process_input_inner handler, and the user gets '[Pi] Error: HTTPSConnectionPool...' — which fails the friendly-indicator assert (and in production means a turn can die because a weather API hiccuped).
where_failed: pi_agent.py awareness_snapshot property first-load branch (no exception guard, unlike the background-refresh branch which IS guarded); test fixture not hermetic (live network inside a unit test).
why_likely: T-041 lazy awareness moved the fetch from startup into the property; the bg-refresh path got a try/except (T-067) but the first-load path never did. The test predates the lazy property and was never made hermetic.
fix_plan: Wrap the first-load branch in try/except: on failure, cache '' (empty snapshot), set _awareness_last_refresh so the TTL bg-refresh retries later, count via _awareness_refresh_failures, track via _track_silent. A turn must NEVER fail because an awareness API is down — empty snapshot degrades gracefully (shortcut returns None, prompt just lacks the block).
Make the test fixture hermetic: pre-seed agent._awareness_snapshot_cache='' and _awareness_last_refresh=now so no live fetch happens inside the unit test.
Add a regression test: awareness.get_awareness_snapshot raising must still produce a normal (friendly) turn.
